steps.title: Use corresponding angles
steps.content: Similar figures have equal corresponding angles. The given scale factor tells how the side lengths compare, but it does not change any angle measures.

steps.title: Match the named vertices
steps.content: The problem says vertex $P$ corresponds to vertex $W$. That means angle $P$ and angle $W$ are a corresponding pair, so they have the same measure in the two similar trapezoids.

steps.title: Find angle W
steps.content: Since angle $P$ measures $48^\circ$, angle $W$ also measures $48^\circ$. The answer is B.
